brief.title: aion-awl hygiene PURGE (finale part A): purge the LAST lint bypasses in the crate by doing the real work — ast.rs 190 missing_docs by real docs, printer.rs vestigial missing_docs deletion (delete-and-confirm), and the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string (55 sites) by Result-returning tests. After this brief crates/aion-awl/ carries ZERO bypasses and the crate-wide zero-bypass grep goes GREEN. Mechanically fenced scope: FIVE owned files, NO CI edit (that is 001e-b). Split from the AWL0-REFAC-001e finale per operator right-sizing ruling.
brief.objective: Purge the last lint-bypass attributes in crates/aion-awl/ by doing the real work, at ZERO behavior cost, touching ONLY five files (ast.rs, printer.rs, the three test files). NO CI edit is in scope — .github/workflows/ci.yml belongs to 001e-b and the scope fence forbids touching it. THREE concerns, each mechanically gated. (1) ast.rs — delete the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and write REAL, informative `///` docs for all 190 missing-doc AST items (authoring-measured on base fab18d23: 16 structs, 118 fields [76 struct fields + 42 enum-variant named fields], 47 enum variants across all 9 enums, 7 undocumented enums, 1 trait `Spanned`, 1 method `span`; docs-only, no signature change). Keep-or-improve the already-documented fields (Document::finish_leading/finish_trailing/epilogue_comments; ActionDecl/StepDecl leading_comments/trailing_comments; HandlerBlock's action_leading/action_trailing/terminal_leading/terminal_trailing; the enum-level docs on ActionFieldTag and StepFieldTag). `pub(crate) fn join_span` is pub(crate) so missing_docs does not fire on it. Placeholder/identifier-restating docs (`The span.`, `The name.`) are a BLOCKING finding. (2) printer.rs — delete the line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` and CONFIRM clippy green; authoring measured this attr VESTIGIAL (printer.rs's only public item `pub fn print` at line 12 already carries a doc at line 10, so deletion leaves clippy green with ZERO new errors). Do NO doc work and NO other edit to printer.rs — a one-line deletion, exactly like 001b's vestigial lib.rs attrs; leave `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs re-export). (3) The three test files (tests/awl2_defects.rs:8, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16, tests/parser_printer.rs:3) — delete each `#![allow(...)]` and eliminate all 55 sites (awl2_defects 32, field_trivia 20, parser_printer 3) by REAL restructuring: `.expect(...)` on Result -> `#[test]` fn returns `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` + `?`; `.expect_err(...)` on Result -> `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("...".into()) };`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect(...)` on Option -> `.ok_or("...")?`; the single `format_push_string` (awl2_defects helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`) -> `use std::fmt::Write as _;` + `let _ = write!(src, ...)`. parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns Result and uses `?` — mirror it. NEVER re-add a bypass, `#[ignore]`, or a `.unwrap_or*` swallow; every test asserts the SAME behaviour. The crate has NO #[ignore]d and NO external-service tests, so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern does not arise. After the three purges the crate-wide zero-bypass grep goes GREEN — that green is this brief's completion signal (wiring it as a permanent CI gate is 001e-b). The full R1-R5 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-a-input.json — read it in the workspace.
brief.context:
Part A of the two-way split of the AWL0-REFAC-001e hygiene finale (operator ruling 2026-07-09: the finale was authored whole but is too heavy for one dispatch — 190 doc sentences + 55 test-site conversions + a novel CI mechanism spanning three skills — so it is dispatched as 001e-a HYGIENE PURGE and 001e-b CI WIRING, with 001e-b depending on 001e-a landing; see BRIEF-CRAFT entry 10 / generalised principle 5). 001e-a owns THE PURGE and makes crates/aion-awl/ zero-bypass a TRUE fact; the CI gate that keeps it permanent forever is 001e-b. Stamped from the 001c/001d templates. 001a (checker split) merged adb6e4cf; 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) merged 80426594; 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) SALVAGED and merged 9800b9c4 (plan status fab18d23); 001d (emitter pure split) preceded this brief. This finale-A purges the LAST bypasses. Its dispatch depends on 001c (whose parser/mod.rs re-export resolves printer.rs's `use crate::parser::duration_text;`) AND 001d (the finale lands after every split is complete). At dispatch crates/aion-awl/src/parser/ and src/emitter/ folder modules exist (parser.rs/emitter.rs gone), 001c already purged parser.rs's line-1 block, and emitter carried no block. This brief's five targets are protected from 001c/001d by their scope fences, so the authoring measurements below (RE-MEASURED on base fab18d2373dd22b2f2794ede8ce02c41d33234d6 = current main after 001c's salvage landing) are EXACT at dispatch.

THIS BRIEF IS PART PURGE-BY-REAL-WORK (ast.rs docs + the three test files) AND PART VESTIGIAL-DELETION (printer.rs) — NO CI wiring (that is 001e-b). It owns the LAST bypasses in the crate. Do NOT hunt for purge work that measurement says does not exist (printer.rs), and do NOT introduce gratuitous edits under cover of the purge.

INTENTION: The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors mid-split of four files at once — so it was decomposed smallest-first. Every non-negotiable here is a mechanical gate (two GREEN on base: lints-pinned, scope-fence; one RED on base BY DESIGN: crate-wide-zero-bypass, because it IS the work — it goes green exactly when the five owned files are purged). The scope fence makes wandering impossible, and the full measured inventory is embedded so the developer neither hunts phantom purge work nor improvises the plan-vs-reality divergences.

CONSTRAINTS:
- CN1: Canonical docs are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 AWL0-REFAC-001e entry (the purge half) is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. The finale's plan-vs-reality divergences were resolved at authoring (printer.rs vestigial; ast.rs 190; the CI gate is checker-fixtures-scoped — the last belongs to 001e-b); discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise.
- CN2: Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses, thiserror in libraries, no unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in library code, tests may return Result and use ?, files <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, no silent failures. #[ignore] is REJECTED; the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` runtime-skip is the only sanctioned alternative for external-service tests — this crate has none, so it does not arise.
- CN3: Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (no-bypasses-crate-wide, lints-pinned, scope-fence) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.
- CN4: Round-trip property is sacred (parse . print = id, byte-level, every fixture) and the emitter .gleam.golden files are byte-pinned; this brief must not perturb any of it (the scope fence and the docs-only/one-line-deletion edits make perturbing it impossible).
- CN5: Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — clause-by-clause against R1-R5, including the docs-quality clause (placeholder ast.rs docs are BLOCKING), that printer.rs was a vestigial delete-and-confirm, and that every test's asserted behaviour is preserved with no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass. All four lenses carry the check-base-before-labeling-regression clause (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16): read the base version before calling a preserved pre-existing behaviour a regression.
- CN6: Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge.

The full R1-R5 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-a-input.json — read it in the workspace.
pointers: docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-input.json (the SUPERSEDED whole finale — retained context for the measured inventory and divergences; note it was split into 001e-a + 001e-b by operator ruling)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001c-input.json (the purge-by-doing-the-work template — docs written, unwrap/expect eliminated)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001b-input.json (the vestigial-attr delete-and-confirm precedent — printer.rs here is the same shape)

docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md (Wave 0 — the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry: the CONTRACT; note it says `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge` and `the three test files' ... purge (tests return Result + ?)`)

docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/BRIEF-CRAFT.md (why the gates are shaped this way; entries 8-19, generalised principles 1-12, principle 10 MEASURE-before-mandating, entry 16 check-base-before-regression, the run a96973cb / 3d201c36 / 15342a09 validation notes)
crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs (delete line-1 attr; write 190 real docs — the whole AWL AST type surface)
crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs (delete line-1 attr; VESTIGIAL — confirm clippy green, no doc work, one-line diff; leave the duration_text import)
crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs, tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs, tests/parser_printer.rs (delete each attr; convert to Result-returning tests — parser_printer.rs already shows the shape)
crates/aion-awl/src/checker/, crates/aion-awl/src/lexer/, crates/aion-awl/src/parser/, crates/aion-awl/src/emitter/ or emitter.rs (the landed/split modules — DO NOT EDIT; the scope fence guards them)
.github/workflows/ci.yml (DO NOT EDIT — 001e-b owns it; the scope fence fails on any edit here)
scope_in: crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs (delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; write real docs for all 190 items; no signature change)
crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs (delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; confirm clippy green — vestigial, one-line diff, no other edit)
crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs (delete line-8 attr; convert 32 sites to Result-returning tests)
crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs (delete line-3 attr; convert 3 sites; the file already uses Result+?)
scope_out: REAL DOCS, NOT PLACEHOLDERS (R1): ast.rs's 190 doc comments must be informative sentences; `The span.`, restating an identifier, or any placeholder is a BLOCKING finding. Docs only — no signature change. Already-documented fields are kept-or-improved, never regressed.

printer.rs IS DELETE-AND-CONFIRM (R2): the attr is vestigial (measured — clippy green after deletion, one already-documented public fn). Delete the single line, confirm clippy green, make NO other edit; do NOT touch `use crate::parser::duration_text;`.

TESTS RETURN Result, NO SWALLOW, NO #[ignore] (R3): eliminate all 55 sites by ?/ok_or/let-else/write!. NEVER re-add a bypass, NEVER #[ignore], NEVER `.unwrap_or*`-swallow. Every test asserts the SAME behaviour. No external-service tests exist, so no env-gate is in play.

NO CI EDIT (001e-a): .github/workflows/ci.yml is 001e-b's exclusive surface. Do NOT edit it; the scope fence forbids it. This brief makes the crate zero-bypass a TRUE fact; 001e-b wires the permanent CI gate.

NO LINT-POLICY WEAKENING ANYWHERE (R4): no bypass introduced in src or tests; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml byte-identical (dispatch 2 of the parent brief was rejected for a private manifest allow-list). The crate-wide zero-bypass grep must be GREEN after this brief.

SCOPE FENCE (R5): the diff touches ONLY the five owned paths. lib.rs, parser/, emitter/, checker/, lexer/, Cargo.toml, ci.yml, fixtures, goldens, canonicals, every other crate are UNMODIFIED. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding.
ZERO behavior change: no AST shape/span/diagnostic/emitted-Gleam/verdict change; the round-trip property and emitter goldens are untouched.
SHALL NOT touch any crate other than aion-awl, nor anything in aion-awl outside the five owned paths, nor .github/workflows/ci.yml.
acceptance: no-bypasses-CRATE-WIDE gate (RED on base BY DESIGN — the headline): `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` — red on base fab18d23 (the five owned files still carry bypasses), green only when every one is purged.
lints-pinned gate (GREEN on base): crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml has exactly one [lints] section, containing workspace = true.
scope-fence gate (GREEN on base): git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding the five owned paths, is empty.

cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean. (The full workspace suite is run by the operator at merge review while tasks #243/#244 are open.)

Hand review: ast.rs docs are REAL sentences (no placeholders) with no signature change; printer.rs is a one-line deletion, clippy green; the three test files return Result with every asserted behaviour intact and no swallow/ignore/re-added bypass; Cargo.toml/lib.rs/parser/emitter/checker/lexer/fixtures/goldens/ci.yml untouched; the crate-wide no-bypasses grep is GREEN.
brief.notes:
DISPATCH HISTORY (read this first): the parent brief AWL0-REFAC-001 was dispatched twice and REJECTED twice at operator review, then decomposed into five one-concern sub-briefs. Dispatch 1 faked the split with include!("parts/part_00.rs") textual splices behind 2-line mod.rs files plus a crate-wide #![allow(...)]. Dispatch 2 rolled the split back after cascading errors and instead replaced aion-awl's Cargo.toml `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list to silence clippy. BOTH escape hatches are now mechanical gates that run before anything expensive; do the real work. The pilots then landed FIRST-PASS clean: 001a (checker split) merged adb6e4cf, 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) merged 80426594 (8/8 gates, 4/4 lenses, zero deviations each); 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) ended cycle_cap_exhausted, was SALVAGED on-branch, and merged 9800b9c4; 001d (emitter pure split) preceded this brief. This finale is stamped from them. Honest reporting was noted favourably every time: if something genuinely cannot be done within the boundaries, say so plainly in your report rather than working around a gate.

THIS IS THE HYGIENE FINALE, PART A (the PURGE) — part real-work purge, part vestigial deletion (measure-before-mandating, BRIEF-CRAFT principle 10). It owns the LAST bypasses in the crate. The CI-wiring half (the awl-check fixtures job + the AWL1-004 carve-out + the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass CI gate) is 001e-b, dispatched after this lands. Do NOT hunt for purge work that measurement says does not exist (printer.rs), do NOT introduce gratuitous edits under cover of the purge, and do NOT touch .github/workflows/ci.yml.

TECHNIQUE: (1) ast.rs — delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`, write real `///` docs for all 190 items (the whole AWL AST type surface). Describe what each type/field/variant IS or LOCATES; for `pub span: Span` say what it spans, for `pub name: String` say what it identifies. Some fields already carry docs (Document::finish_leading/finish_trailing/epilogue_comments; ActionDecl/StepDecl leading_comments/trailing_comments; HandlerBlock's action_leading/action_trailing/terminal_leading/terminal_trailing) and the two enum-level docs (ActionFieldTag, StepFieldTag) — keep or improve them, never regress. No signature change. (2) printer.rs — delete line-1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`, run clippy, confirm green. It is VESTIGIAL: printer.rs's only public item `pub fn print(document: &Document) -> String` (line 12) already carries `/// Render an AWL document in the canonical AWL-0 format.` (line 10). This is a one-line diff, exactly like 001b's four vestigial lib.rs attrs — no doc-writing, no other edit; leave the `use crate::parser::duration_text;` import (line 8, resolves through 001c's parser/mod.rs re-export). (3) The three test files — delete each attr, convert to Result-returning tests. parser_printer.rs ALREADY returns `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` and uses `?` in most tests — mirror that shape. Site classes and fixes: `.expect(...)` on Result -> return Result + `?`; `.expect_err(...)` on Result (assert parse FAILS) -> `let Err(error) = parse(source) else { return Err("<why>".into()) };` then inspect `error`; `.unwrap()`/`.expect(...)` on Option (`.find(...)`, `.nth(1).map(...)`, `.as_ref()`, a `match{...}` -> Option) -> `.ok_or("<what was missing>")?`; the single format_push_string (awl2_defects helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`, `src.push_str(&format!(...))`) -> `use std::fmt::Write as _;` + `let _ = write!(src, ...)`. NEVER `.unwrap_or*`-swallow, NEVER re-add a bypass, NEVER `#[ignore]`.

MEASURED INVENTORY (authoring, RE-MEASURED on base fab18d2373dd22b2f2794ede8ce02c41d33234d6 = current main after 001c's salvage landing). FULL crate bypass grep (`grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/`) returns EXACTLY FIVE sites, ALL owned by this brief: crates/aion-awl/src/ast.rs:1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]`; crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs:1 `#![allow(missing_docs)]` (VESTIGIAL); crates/aion-awl/tests/awl2_defects.rs:8 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::format_push_string)]`; crates/aion-awl/tests/field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs:16 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::format_push_string)]`; crates/aion-awl/tests/parser_printer.rs:3 `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::unwrap_used)]`. parser.rs's line-1 block is GONE (001c salvage landed, merge 9800b9c4); there is NO `#[ignore]` and NO `include!(` anywhere; emitter carries no block. So purging these five turns the crate-wide grep green — that is the completion signal. PER-TARGET FALLOUT: (a) ast.rs block deleted -> exactly 190 missing_docs items, ALL in ast.rs, NO other lint. Structural breakdown (verified by reading ast.rs): 16 structs (Document, Comment, Trivia, WorkflowDecl, AboutDecl, IoDecl, TypeDecl, FieldDecl, ActionDecl, BindDecl, StepDecl, EachSpec, CallExpr, HandlerBlock, DurationLiteral, RecordField); 118 fields = 76 named struct fields + 42 named fields inside enum variants (StepOp::Wait{span,signal}=2, CallTarget::Child{span,workflow,args}=3, RetrySpec::Every{span,count,every}=3 + Backoff{span,count,min,max}=4, TypeRef::Named/List/Option=2+2+2, Expr's variant bodies=24 total); 47 enum variants across ALL nine enums (ActionFieldTag 4, StepFieldTag 11, StepOp 3, CallTarget 2, HandlerTerminal 2, RetrySpec 2, TypeRef 3, Expr 11, BinaryOp 9 — a variant needs its own doc even when its enum is documented); 7 undocumented enums (StepOp, CallTarget, HandlerTerminal, RetrySpec, TypeRef, Expr, BinaryOp — ActionFieldTag and StepFieldTag ALREADY carry enum-level docs); 1 trait `Spanned`; 1 method `fn span`. 16+118+47+7+1+1 = 190. Docs-only; no unwrap/expect/too_many_lines/unnecessary_wraps; no public-fn signature pressure. `pub(crate) fn join_span` is pub(crate) — missing_docs does not fire on it (not part of the 190). (b) printer.rs block deleted -> ZERO clippy errors (VESTIGIAL): printer.rs has exactly ONE public item, `pub fn print` (line 12), already documented (line 10) — clippy stays GREEN. Delete-and-confirm, no doc work. (c) three test files' attrs deleted -> 55 sites: awl2_defects.rs 32 (10 expect_err, 13 expect, 8 unwrap, 1 push_str(&format!)), field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs 20 (5 expect_err, 13 expect, 2 unwrap), parser_printer.rs 3 (1 expect_err, 2 unwrap). Lint composition crate-wide: 16 `expect_err()` on Result, 16 `expect()` on Result, 10 `expect()` on Option, 11 `unwrap()` on Option, 1 `unwrap()` on Result, 1 `format_push_string` (awl2_defects.rs helper `doc_with_broken_expr_at_step`). ALL are production test paths. field_trivia_and_duplicates.rs's attr lists `clippy::format_push_string` but that file has NO such site (that portion of its allow is vestigial); deleting the whole line is still correct. The crate has NO #[ignore]d tests and NO external-service tests, so the env-gate/`*_TEST_URL` pattern does not arise.

DIVERGENCES (plan vs measured reality — resolved at authoring, stated as findings per CN1): (1) The plan says `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge`, implying BOTH need doc-writing. MEASURED: printer.rs's attr is VESTIGIAL (delete-and-confirm, zero docs) while ast.rs is 190 real doc items. Resolved: R1 = ast.rs real-docs purge; R2 = printer.rs vestigial deletion (like 001b). (2) The plan does not quantify ast.rs; it is 190 items (the whole AST surface) — embedded as the R1 inventory so the developer starts fully informed. (3) [BELONGS TO 001e-b, recorded here for completeness] the plan's `wire aion awl check over every committed .awl fixture EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures` resolves to the CHECKER-FIXTURE set crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl (five files, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures fail); the two aspirational AWL-1 example docs (approval_gate.awl, sized_fanout.awl) are documentation ahead of the implementation and out of scope — but that whole concern is 001e-b's, not this brief's.

SIZING (why this is 001e-a, not the whole finale): the whole AWL0-REFAC-001e finale spanned THREE distinct skills — 190 AST doc sentences under a no-placeholder bar (R1), 55 test-site conversions across three files (R3), and a novel CI mechanism with a self-retiring expected-failure carve-out (R4/R5 of the finale). Authoring judged that EXCEEDED the one-dev-round + 3-fix-cycle envelope and RECOMMENDED the split; the operator RULED to split into 001e-a (this brief — the purge) and 001e-b (CI wiring, dependent on this landing). 001e-a is still the heavier of the two — 190 docs + 55 conversions — but it is now single-surface (five files, one skill family: purge-by-real-work) and fits the envelope. If a fix cycle is consumed by the ast.rs docs, report an honest partial rather than a rushed one — the operator would rather redispatch than merge placeholder docs.

GATES: all three mechanical gates were designed against the base ref. TWO are GREEN ON BASE (any red in them is yours): lints-pinned (exit 0), scope-fence (exit 0 on the clean tree; correctly exit 1 on a synthetic out-of-scope edit; exit 0 on a synthetic in-scope edit to any of the five owned files). ONE is RED ON BASE BY DESIGN because it IS the work: no-bypasses-CRATE-WIDE (exit 1 on base fab18d23 — the five owned files still carry bypasses; verified it FLIPS to exit 0 when all five bypass lines are removed). The scope-fence gate is pinned to a base SHA: the literal string PINNED_SHA_AT_DISPATCH in the scope-fence argv MUST be replaced by the operator with `git rev-parse main` at dispatch time — 001d (and possibly other work) will have merged since authoring, so main WILL have moved and the pin MUST be refreshed; the operator re-verifies the two green-on-base gates green + the one red-on-base gate red on that fresh base before dispatching. The five owned files are protected from 001d by its scope fence (001d touches only emitter*), so the measured inventory stays exact across the re-pin. The test gates are scoped (aion-awl + aion-cli) and deterministic — the flaky workspace suite is deliberately NOT a gate here (tasks #243/#244: aion-package `append_run_regenerates_type_checking_gleam` and aion-server --lib both flake under load in crates this diff never touches); if a workspace test outside aion-awl/aion-cli ever runs and flakes while every in-scope gate passes and your diff touches only the five owned paths, re-run once and report the flake honestly rather than attempting an out-of-scope fix. The operator runs the full workspace suite by hand at merge review. NOTE: there is NO no-bypasses-scoped-companion gate in this brief's set — the crate-wide no-bypasses gate SUBSUMES it (it scans all of crates/aion-awl/, so any regression in a landed path keeps it red too), and only paths that EXIST are named in the one grep (crates/aion-awl/ exists), avoiding the ugrep exit-2 false-green trap.

gates.name: lints-pinned
argv: test "$(grep -c '^\[lints' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml)" -eq 1 && grep -A1 '^\[lints\]$' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml | grep -q '^workspace = true'

lenses.name: correctness
lenses.charter: Hunt defects in the change itself: logic errors, unhandled cases, broken invariants, silent failure paths. The highest-value defect classes here: (1) a test-side unwrap/expect elimination that changed which branch runs or which case is asserted — an `expect_err` converted to a let-else that no longer proves parse FAILED, an `.ok_or(...)?` whose error path is now reachable when the value was actually always present, a `?` that short-circuits a test before its assertions; (2) an ast.rs doc that is subtly WRONG about what a field means (a doc is a claim — verify it against the field's use in parser/printer/checker/emitter). Assume the change is wrong and construct the input or state that proves it. Before labeling a defect a REGRESSION, read the base version of the code (`git show <base>:<file>`) — a faithfully preserved pre-existing bug/behaviour is a finding-for-follow-up, not a blocking rejection (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16). Placeholder or uninformative ast.rs docs (`The span.`, restating an identifier) are a BLOCKING finding, not a nit.

lenses.name: regressions
lenses.charter: Hunt collateral damage: behaviour the diff changes that the brief did not ask to change. Every test must assert the SAME thing it did on base_ref — same source parses/fails identically, same span/line/column/message assertions hold; a test that now passes for a different reason (or no longer exercises the failure it was written to guard) is a regression. The round-trip property (parse . print = id) and the emitter .gleam.golden files must be untouched (the scope fence forbids editing fixtures/goldens, but verify no ast.rs/printer.rs edit perturbed emitted output). Watch for a gratuitous edit smuggled under the purge (a printer.rs change beyond the one-line attr deletion, an ast.rs signature/field change, a test whose assertions were weakened to make the ?-conversion compile). Any fixture/golden/canonical edit or any ci.yml edit is an automatic finding (ci.yml is 001e-b's). Before labeling a preserved pre-existing behaviour a regression, read the base version first (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16): a faithfully carried-over bug is a follow-up finding, not a blocking rejection.

lenses.name: brief_compliance
lenses.charter: Hold the diff to the brief, adversarially: every acceptance criterion either demonstrably met or a blocking finding; every scope_out boundary respected; every claimed deviation actually declared. Pass-and-still-wrong is the failure class — do not accept a claim without evidence in the diff or gate output. Confirm specifically: R1 ast.rs docs are REAL (spot-check for placeholders — `The span.`, restating an identifier — which are BLOCKING) and no signature changed; R2 printer.rs is a ONE-LINE deletion with clippy green and no other edit; R3 zero unwrap/expect/expect_err remain in the three test files with no `.unwrap_or*` swallow, no `#[ignore]`, no re-added bypass; R4 the crate-wide grep is green and Cargo.toml is byte-identical; R5 only the five owned paths changed (NO ci.yml edit — that is 001e-b). Placeholder docs are BLOCKING; check-base-before-regression applies (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).

lenses.name: spec_fidelity
lenses.charter: Read AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 (the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry — `ast.rs + printer.rs missing_docs purge, the three test files' expect_used/unwrap_used/format_push_string purge (tests return Result + ?) ... the crate-wide zero-bypass gate goes green here and stays a permanent gate`) and the committed brief docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-a-input.json, and check the implementation clause by clause against R1-R5. This brief is finale PART A (the PURGE); the CI-wiring half (the awl-check fixtures job + the permanent crate-wide CI gate) is 001e-b — so ci.yml is OUT of this brief's scope and any ci.yml edit is a finding. (R1) ast.rs's line-1 allow gone and all 190 items carry REAL informative docs — placeholder/uninformative docs (`The span.`, restating the identifier) are a BLOCKING finding; no signature change; already-documented fields not regressed. (R2) printer.rs's line-1 allow gone via a delete-and-confirm — the plan implied a doc purge but authoring measured it VESTIGIAL (clippy green after deletion, one already-documented public fn), so verify NO gratuitous edit and NO doc churn was smuggled in. (R3) the three test files carry zero bypass attrs and zero unwrap/expect/expect_err, converted to Result-returning tests via ?/ok_or/let-else/write! with every asserted behaviour preserved — no swallow, no #[ignore], no re-added bypass, no invented behaviour. (R4) the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is GREEN; crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml [lints] is byte-identical (workspace = true only); clippy -D warnings green. (R5) the diff touches ONLY the five owned paths. ANY lint-policy weakening (source attr OR manifest OR a relocated bypass), any placeholder doc, any unwrap/expect left or swallowed, any #[ignore], any fixture/golden edit, any ci.yml edit, or any change outside the fence is an automatic blocking finding — both prior dispatches of the parent brief were rejected for the fraud patterns. Before calling a preserved pre-existing behaviour a regression, read the base version first (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).
